title: Create submission tracker for query rounds, agent replies, and revision follow-ups
description:
## Problem
After manuscript completion, writers lose context across submissions (who was queried, when responses arrived, what changes were requested). Spreadsheets and inbox threads break continuity and reduce follow-through.

## Target user
Writers preparing to query agents, submit to contests, or run structured beta-reader rounds.

## Outcome / KPI
- 35% of users who export a submission package also create at least one tracked submission entry.
- 20% increase in completed follow-up actions within target response windows.
- Reduction in missed response deadlines and duplicate submissions.

## Scope
- Add submission tracker workspace tied to a manuscript.
- Track entries with status, recipient, sent date, response date, response type, and next action date.
- Provide timeline and board views (e.g., Drafting, Submitted, Requested Revisions, Closed).
- Add reminder nudges for overdue follow-ups and pending requested revisions.
- Link each submission entry to exported package version or manuscript snapshot used at send time.

## Non-goals
- Automated email sending from the app.
- Marketplace/discovery functionality for publishers or agents.
- Contract/legal workflow management.

## Acceptance criteria
- User can create, edit, and archive submission entries per manuscript.
- Board/timeline views accurately reflect submission status transitions.
- Reminder state appears for overdue follow-up dates.
- Entry can reference a specific export/snapshot artifact so writer knows exactly what was sent.
- Data persists and is included in manuscript-level backups.

## Priority rationale
This extends the product from drafting into real publishing workflow outcomes, reducing drop-off after completion. It complements the existing package builder while creating a practical end-to-end moat for serious writers.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
